sections.title: Talking to Otto for the first time

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Good day! You seem a hearty warrior. Maybe even some barbarian blood in that body of yours?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You think so?

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Who can tell? My forefathers weren't averse to travelling, so it is possible. They tended to cause too much trouble in your so-called civilised lands, however, so most returned to their ancestral homes.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: In any case, given you seem the type, I think you are ready to learn our more secret tribal feats for yourself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, that sounds interesting. What sort of skills would these be?

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: To begin with, I can supply knowledge in the ways of firemaking, our special rod fishing tricks, a selection of spear skills and our agricultural techniques.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There are more advanced stages though, going by your description?

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Your perception is creditable. I can eventually teach of more advanced firemaking techniques, and the rod fishing skills are but a preliminary to our special potions and brews.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: These secrets must, however, wait until you have mastered the basics.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better get going.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you and why do you live all the way out here?

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Me? I am Otto, known as the Godblessed. I follow the ways of our barbarian ancestors, communing with the spirits of the dead and teaching their ways to worthy disciples.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Perhaps you could be one of them. You look a likely sort to learn of these ways. As I say, you might even have a bit of barbarian blood hidden away in you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I really don't think I'm related to any barbarian.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Your scepticism will be your loss.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, I'm too busy to talk about genealogy today.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Well then, we will talk when you learn to be less impetuous.

sections.title: Talking to Otto again without doing as he asks

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Good day to you, hearty warrior! May the spirits guide you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please teach me of your cunning with harpoons.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: I see you need encouragement in learning the ways of fishing without a harpoon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's not the most safe-sounding task I have ever been given.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Neither is it the most deadly. Go now and return when you have drawn a fish from the sea, with sheer strength and knowledge of our scaled prey.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can I learn about the use of a fishing rod?

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Alas, I do not sense that you have been successful in your fishing yet. The look in your eyes is not that of the osprey.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Osprey?

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Legendary birds, which the ignorant call eagles. They prey upon fish. Do as they do to gain inspiration.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm ready for your firemaking wisdom. Please instruct me.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: By now you know my response.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Go and light some oak logs using a strung bow?

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Exactly.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: I see you have yet to be successful in planting a seed with your fists.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's definitely one of the more delicate tasks you have provided me.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Remember to be calm, and good luck.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better get going.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Good day to you, hearty warrior! May the spirits guide you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please teach me of your cunning with harpoons.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: To learn more of such as tool, you must first focus your studies on those fish that the tool is used to catch.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: You must catch fish which are usually harpooned, without a harpoon. You will be using your skill and strength.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you expect me to do that?

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Use your arm as bait. Wriggle your fingers as if they are a tasty snack and hungry tuna and swordfish will throng to be caught by you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That sounds rather insanely dangerous. I'm glad you didn't mention shark too.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Oh, my mind slipped for a moment, this method does indeed work with shark - though in this case the action must be more of a frenzied thrashing of the arm than a wriggle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and I thought fishing was a safe way to pass the time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I seek more knowledge.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have no more questions at this time.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: In that case, farewell.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can I learn about the use of a fishing rod?

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: While you civilised folk use small, weak fishing rods, we barbarians are skilled with heavier tackle. We fish in the lake nearby.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So can you teach me of your methods?

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Certainly. Take the rod from under my bed and fish in the lake. When you have caught a few fish, I am sure you will be ready to talk more with me.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: You will know when you are ready, since inspiration will fill your mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I can obtain new foods from these fishing spots?

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: We do not use these fish quite as you might expect. When you return we can speak more of this matter.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm ready for your firemaking wisdom. Please instruct me.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: The first point in your progression is that of lighting fires without a tinderbox.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That sounds pretty useful. Tell me more.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: For this process you will require a strung bow. You use the bow to quickly rotate pieces of wood against one another. As you rub the wood, it becomes hot, eventually springing into flame.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No more secret details?

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: The spirits will aid you. The power they supply will guide your hands. Go and benefit from their guidance upon oak logs.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can I use my strength in the ways of agriculture?

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Ah, so you wish to get your hands dirty? I can teach you how to plant seeds without the use of a seed dibber.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can handle a little bit of muck. How does this work?

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: The difficulty in this method is providing enough force to deeply root the seed in the ground, yet with enough care so that the the seed itself is not damaged.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Practice this. You might fail at first, especially if like many a barbarian, you are not used to holding back. Indeed, it may be wise to avoid using seeds you are not willing to lose.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll do my best.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Remember to be calm, and good luck.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better get going.

sections.title: Speaking to Otto after learning of pyre ships

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Good day to you, hearty warrior! May the spirits guide you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's talk about my training.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have anything for me?

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: I see you have free space and no way to record any information you may recover from the caverns. Please take this book as a record of your researches.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What kind of information are you thinking of?

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Those who have previously entered the cavern have not all returned. Some of them seemed intent upon research and discovery as much as loot and daring deeds.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: It would be a shame if you were to recover any of their notes and be unable to record them handily. I hope the new book will give you space for such discoveries.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you tell me more of these spirits you continually refer to?

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Certainly, though it is quite simple - they are the barbarians of the past who have died in the passing of the years. There are three categories which learned sages claim may describe all spirits encountered.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: The majority are at peace, having died with their ambitions sated, even if this ambition was as simple as a glorious death. They can contact those in our world in order to spur us on to glory.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Of course, they cannot or will not contact all inhabitants of these lands, for not all are worthy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So those are the spirits who give us guidance. What of the other sorts?

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: A second category of spirit is made up of those who are not yet at peace. However, all that is needed is for their mortal remains to be laid to rest and they will join the peaceful majority.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can see the pyres serve this purpose. The final category is also restless?

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Alas, there is a small minority who will never be at rest. These have been slain in moments of insanity by their brothers or by adventurers.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: They are not at rest and will attack those they blame for their tortured state. They are not known, however, for being able to determine this blame with any great accuracy, so should be considered dangerous to all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you Otto. It makes a bit more sense now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better get going.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Good day to you, hearty warrior! May the spirits guide you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've fished with my hands!

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: I sense you have more understanding of harpoons through your studies.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well it was interesting, can I move on to more sane hobbies now?

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Nothing is sane when one is born knowing only certain death. The spirits approve of you however, so death is on your side.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is that supposed to mean?

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: I mean that when you eventually die and find peace, at least the spirits you encounter will be your friends. Alas for you adventurous sort, the natural ways of passing are close to impossible.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've fished with a barbarian rod!

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Your mind is as clear as the waters you have fished. This is good.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what do I do with these fish? They are all bloated and so nasty looking that even a starving vulture wouldn't eat one.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Patience young one. These are fish which are fat with eggs rather than fat of flesh. It is these eggs that are the thing to make use of.

sections.title: Speaking to Otto after creating a pyre ship

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Good day to you, hearty warrior! May the spirits guide you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's talk about my training.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've created a pyre ship!

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Hail to you, saviour of my ancestors. The spirits herald your presence with a spectral fanfare.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's good to be appreciated.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: On this great day you have my eternal thanks. May you find riches while rescuing my spiritual ancestors in the caverns for many moons to come.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: And with that, your training is done. You have gone from apprentice, to master. Seek to inspire all with your daring deeds and legendary feats.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Good day to you, hearty warrior! May the spirits guide you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've set fire to logs with a bow!

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Fine news indeed!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have completed firemaking with a bow. What follows this?

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: The next stage is quite complex, so listen well. In order to send our ancestors into the spirit world, their mortal remains must be burned with due ceremony.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: This can only be done close to the water on the shore of the lake, just to our north-east. You will recognise the correct places by the ashes to be seen there.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: You will need to construct a small ship by using an axe upon logs in this area, then add the bones of a long dead barbarian hero.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do I obtain these bones?

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: From the caverns beneath this lake. Many of our ancestors travelled to these caverns in order to hunt for glory, but found only death. Their bones must still lie inside, their spirits trapped in torment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I make sure I am in the right place, then throw these bones on a ceremonial ship before I set light to it?

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Correct. The spirit will ascend to glory while the pyre will send the earthly remains to the depths. You will also obtain a closer link to the spirit world.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: During this heightened contact, any bones you bury will have increased importance to the gods. The number of bones that may be buried before the link fades will depend on the wood used.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there anything else I should know?

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: I have little knowledge of the caverns - they are blocked from the sight of the spirits with whom I commune. I can only suspect that whatever slew the barbarian heroes is indeed mighty.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: I also suspect that the bones you seek might well be very uncommon, since heroes are not found in vast numbers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I enter the caverns?

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Dive into the whirlpool in the lake to the east. The spirits will use their abilities to ensure you arrive in the correct location. Be warned, their influence fades, so you must find your own way out.

sections.title: Speaking to Otto again without making a spear

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Good day to you, hearty warrior! May the spirits guide you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me more about the use of spears.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: You do not exude the presence of one who has poured his soul into manufacturing spears.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The spirits told you?

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Indeed they did. There are no secrets from those who live eternally.

sections.title: Speaking to Otto again without making a hasta

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Good day to you, hearty warrior! May the spirits guide you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What of the one handed spears of which you spoke?

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Take some wood and metal and make a spear upon the nearby anvil, then you may return to me. As an example, you may use bronze bars with normal logs or iron bars with oak logs. If you perform this task I will

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: be freed from the spirits complaining of your lack of dedication.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back.

sections.title: Speaking to Otto again after making a spear

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Good day to you, hearty warrior! May the spirits guide you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me more about the use of spears.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: The manufacture of spears is now yours as a speciality. Use your skill well. In addition I am ready to reveal one-handed spear crafts.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This sounds interesting indeed.

sections.title: Speaking to Otto again after making a hasta

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Good day to you, hearty warrior! May the spirits guide you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What of the one handed spears of which you spoke?

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: I see you have constructed your hasta, and are approaching readiness.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If I may ask, readiness for what?

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: To live life to it's fullest of course - that you may be a peaceful spirit when your time ends.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Good day to you, hearty warrior! May the spirits guide you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me more about the use of spears.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: The next step is to manufacture a spear, suitable for combat. To do so, you must speak with our distant cousins on Karamja, in the village of Tai Bwo Wannai.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Couldn't you just take a bribe or something? I am sure you could do with some cash.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: I am afraid this is a vital step. The spirits foresee that your understanding of spears will increase through your journey there. You may not progress in the use of spears until you have completed this mission.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Many warriors complain that spears are difficult to find, we barbarians thus forge our own.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I just use some metal on an anvil, or is there something else involved?

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: If you use our special barbarian anvil here, you will find it ideal. Other anvils are not sturdy enough or shaped appropriately for the forging work involved. Make any of our spears and return.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Note well that you will require wood for the spear shafts; the quality of wood must be similar to that of the metal involved.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What of the one handed spears of which you spoke?

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: The next step is to manufacture a one-handed version of a spear, suitable for combat. Such a spear is known to us as a hasta.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can't I just pick one up and use it in one hand?

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: As you might suspect, our ways require greater understanding than is gained by simply looking at a weapon. It is also the case that the process involves a differently

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: balanced spear. Before you may use such a weapon in anger, you must make an example. Only then will you fully understand the poise and techniques involved.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I must manufacture one? I see. This makes sense.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Indeed. You may use our special anvil for this spear type too. The ways of black and dragon spears are beyond our knowledge, however; these spears may not be created.

sections.title: Speaking to Otto again without an attack mix

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Good day to you, hearty warrior! May the spirits guide you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was that secret knowledge of herblore we talked of?

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Do you have my potion?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was it you needed again?

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Bring me a lesser attack potion combined with fish roe. There is more importance in this than you will ever know.

sections.title: Speaking to Otto with an attack mix

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Good day to you, hearty warrior! May the spirits guide you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've made a barbarian potion!

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: I will take that off your hands now. I will say no more than that I am eternally grateful.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I feel I am missing some vital information about your need for this potion, though I often have this suspicion.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: I will not reveal all of my private matters to you. Some secrets are best kept rather than revealed.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Good day to you, hearty warrior! May the spirits guide you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was that secret knowledge of herblore we talked of?

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: The secret is linked to the fish in the lake nearby. If you use your knife upon them, several new fishy items will be produced. Fish parts can be used as bait, but the roe and caviar is more useful for us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do I cook them?

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Certainly not. Mixing these items with two dose potions is what should be done. This results in a nutritious slop, perfect for healing as well as imparting the effect of the potion.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Roe can only be used for some of the more easily combined mixes, while caviar may be used for any of the mixes of which I am aware.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What if I only have four dose potions?

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: You are able to decant a four dose potion into an empty vial, thus giving two potions of two doses. This will aid you in the process.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess you just want me to go away and make one of these potions?

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Have I become so predictable? But yes, I do indeed require a potion. It is of the highest importance that you bring me a lesser attack potion combined with fish roe.

sections.title: Speaking to Otto again without fisting in a tree

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Good day to you, hearty warrior! May the spirits guide you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are there any other fist related farming activities I can learn?

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: You have not yet attempted to plant a tree. Why not?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've been busy doing other stuff.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: You know your task. Come back when you have completed it.

sections.title: Speaking to Otto again after fisting in a tree

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Good day to you, hearty warrior! May the spirits guide you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've smashed a pot whilst farming!

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: I can see in your spirit that you have been successful in your latest endeavour.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I planted a tree and got really angry. The pot was gone before I even realised.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: It will become more natural with practice.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: And with that, your training is done. You have gone from apprentice, to master. Seek to inspire all with your daring deeds and legendary feats.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to be calmer and not smash my pots.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Consider it done.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to smash my pots when planting trees again.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Consider it done.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Good day to you, hearty warrior! May the spirits guide you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've planted seeds with my fists!

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Yes, I sensed you had found success. Well done.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It definitely tested my patience.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Patience comes alongside a strong spirit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I hope you're not saying I'm weak?

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: No child, but we all have potential to improve our strength.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are there any other fist related farming activities I can learn?

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: This technique fully embraces your strength as a barbarian. Nuance is not required here. It's about the fine art of smashing pots.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Smashing pots? That doesn't sound overly difficult.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: The secret is how to smash them in a way that doesn't leave debris on the ground, for that would not be of benefit to the environment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That makes sense. I wouldn't want to be littering shards of pot everywhere. What's the trick?

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: Planting a tree is strenuous work. You need to channel this energy into pure anger. Release all that anger on the pot that contained it, shattering it into dust!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm good at getting angry. I'll give it a try.

lines.speaker: Otto Godblessed
lines.text: May the spirits guide you into success.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better get going.
